issue: "every 3 months" loses the anchor (which 3 months). The set is Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct; clearer as "in every 3rd month from January" or listing the months.

When both day-of-month and day-of-week are restricted cron fires when EITHER matches. "on the last day of the month, and on Friday" reads as a conjunction; it must be "on the last day of the month OR on Friday".
dom (15) and dow (FRI) both restricted = OR, but "on day 15 of the month, and on Friday" implies AND. Fix: "on day 15 of the month OR on Friday".
dom (L) and dow (Sun) both restricted = OR, but "on the last day of the month, and on Sunday" implies AND. Fix: use "or".

dom (1) and dow (MON#2) both restricted = OR. The bare comma "on day 1 of the month, on the second Monday of the month" reads as AND/list, not the required EITHER-matches. Fix: "on day 1 of the month OR on the second Monday".

Two faults: (1) dom (31) and dow (Sat,Sun) both restricted = OR, but "and on Saturday and Sunday" implies AND. (2) "Minutes 0 through 30 past the hour, at 09:00 AM and 05:00 PM" is contradictory — the ":00" times clash with the 0–30 minute span; should read "minutes 0 through 30 past the 9 AM and 5 PM hours".
dom (1) and dow (MON) both restricted = OR, but "on day 1 of the month, and on Monday" implies AND. Fix: use "or". (Minor: "every 45 minutes" for */45 actually fires only at :00 and :45.)

dom (15W) and dow (MON#2) both restricted = OR, but the comma-joined "on the weekday nearest day 15..., on the second Monday..." reads as conjunction. Fix: "...nearest day 15 OR on the second Monday".
dom (L) and dow (5L) both restricted = OR, but "on the last day of the month, on the last Friday of the month" (comma) reads as AND. Fix: use "or".
dom (31) and dow (Sun) both restricted = OR, but "on day 31 of the month, and on Sunday" implies AND. Fix: use "or". (Time portion "09:30 AM and 05:30 PM" is correct.)
dom (15W) and dow (MON-FRI) both restricted = OR, but comma-joined "on the weekday nearest day 15..., Monday through Friday" reads as conjunction. Fix: "...nearest day 15 OR Monday through Friday".
dom (1) and dow (MON#2) both restricted = OR, but the comma "on day 1 of the month, on the second Monday of the month" reads as AND. Fix: use "or".
dom (1-5) and dow (MON) both restricted = OR, but "between day 1 and 5 of the month, and on Monday" implies AND. Fix: "between day 1 and 5 of the month OR on Monday".
dom (1) and dow (MON-FRI) both restricted = OR, but comma "on day 1 of the month, Monday through Friday" reads as conjunction. Fix: "on day 1 of the month OR Monday through Friday".
dom (15W) and dow (MON#2) both restricted = OR, but comma "on the weekday nearest day 15..., on the second Monday..." reads as conjunction. Fix: use "or".
